reviews.review_title: Interesting and fun but some issues
reviews.review_description:
First things first I did very much enjoy my time with this game and I definitely bought it just because Lzzy Hale is in it. There are a few things that I would like to see improved/fixed though.

1. The influencer cards that pop up when you first see them really don't last long enough to fully read through. It would be nice to have a way to browse them in game.

2. Maps sometimes lack direction and decent signposting. One of the only maps I got stuck on for a bit was very early in the game and was because one of the sides was a ramp, however because it was on the other side of a fence to a wall and looked very similar I just couldn't tell until I played around jumping in every direction.

3. Arcade mode is bugged. Beating the first set of rounds and getting to level 2 softlocks the game as the next round never starts. You also can't quit and keep your score which is probably why so many of the scores in Arcade mode are very very low.

Overall I did enjoy this game a bunch even if the combat is a bit shallow. The puzzles and platforming itself was fun and the tongue in cheek humour and voice acting was very enjoyable. I'm looking forward to Vol. 2 and hope my issues are addressed.

This was the first platformer I have played in VR and LOVED it. It's fun, it's funny, and a bit challenging. I will admit... I am (very) biased because I am one of the voices in the game. But hey, it's a fun punk rock platformer that utilizes VR in its fullest.
For $5.99 don't expect too much but its worth it for some clever puzzles, funny voice acting and cool music.
